vulnerabilities.description: XSS vulnerability: marked parser renders HTML without sanitization. The mdToHtml() function uses marked.parse() without any HTML escaping or content sanitization, allowing malicious scripts in markdown to execute when rendered with dangerouslySetInnerHTML.
XSS injection point: chapter.bodyHtml rendered with dangerouslySetInnerHTML without prior sanitization. If markdown contains script tags or event handlers, they will execute in the browser.
XSS injection point: keyIdeas rendered with dangerouslySetInnerHTML without sanitization. Inline markdown from chapter data could inject malicious HTML.
XSS injection point: inPractice items rendered with dangerouslySetInnerHTML without sanitization. Same risk as keyIdeas.
Predictable session IDs: Using Date.now() for session IDs is predictable. Should use cryptographically secure random values (crypto.randomBytes or randomUUID).
Silent failure on localStorage errors: Catch-all error handler silently ignores all errors. Could mask corruption issues or security-relevant failures. Consider logging critical errors.

Potential path traversal: Keys are used directly in file operations without validation. If keys contain '../' or special characters, could escape intended directory (low risk as keys controlled by app code, not user input).

recommendations: CRITICAL: Install and use a sanitization library (DOMPurify or @leodido/xss) to sanitize markdown HTML before rendering. Add to dependencies: npm install dompurify @types/dompurify
CRITICAL: Update markdown.tsx to sanitize output: import DOMPurify from 'dompurify'; return DOMPurify.sanitize(marked.parse(src))
CRITICAL: Replace all dangerouslySetInnerHTML usages in Reader.tsx lines 274, 280, 287 with sanitized content or safe alternatives
Use uuid or crypto.randomUUID() instead of Date.now() for session IDs in session.js line 20
Add Content Security Policy (CSP) headers to Vite config to mitigate XSS impact: script-src 'self', no inline scripts
Add input validation for book content keys in memory.js to prevent directory traversal (sanitize against '..' and special chars)
Replace silent catch handlers in store.ts with at least console.warn() for debugging localStorage failures
Consider using a markdown library with built-in sanitization (like markdown-it with sanitization plugins) instead of marked if marked doesn't support it
Add security tests to validate that malicious markdown (e.g., '', '', '
